hero.headline: Natural colors and ingredients for the food and beverage industry
hero.subheadline: A complete range of plant-based natural colorants and the functional ingredients we develop from the vine. Natural-origin solutions, ready for your product.

gama.headline: A complete palette of natural colors
gama.body: From red to blue, from yellow to purple: a natural palette ready to replace synthetic colorants in your products.

problem.headline: Viticulture generates much more than wine.
problem.body: Grape skins, seeds, and stems concentrate the most biologically potent compounds of the entire plant: anthocyanins, polyphenols, dietary fiber, and essential fatty acids. Qualab brings that wealth to new industries — functional foods, beverages, cosmetics, and dietary supplements — expanding the impact of every harvest.

ingredients.headline: Natural actives from viticultural origin
ingredients.body: We transform vine by-products into natural-origin solutions for food, beverages and new industrial applications.

colorante.name: Natural Grape Colorant
colorante.desc: Stabilized anthocyanins from red grape skins. The red of the grape, ready for industry.

aceite.name: Grape Seed Oil
aceite.desc: Cold-pressed oil, rich in essential fatty acids and natural vitamin E.

polvo.name: Grape Skin Powder
polvo.desc: Dehydrated grape skin, a natural source of polyphenols and dietary fiber.

diferencial.headline: Why Qualab
diferencial.d1_body: Raw material from high-altitude Mendoza vineyards. Complete traceability from field to ingredient.
diferencial.d2_body: Validated evidence in collaboration with UC Davis, Catena Institute and research centers.
diferencial.d3_body: Leo, our artificial intelligence platform, compresses R&D cycles and connects science with market.
diferencial.d4_body: We work with you from hypothesis to product. Real speed, focus and flexibility.

que_hacemos.headline: At the intersection of viticulture, science and market
que_hacemos.body: At Qualab we work at the intersection of viticulture, science and new product development. We identify opportunities in vine biomass and transform them into natural-origin functional ingredients, with concrete applications across industries.
que_hacemos.b1_body: Evidence-based technical backing. We work with academic institutions to validate every ingredient before taking it to market.
que_hacemos.b2_body: We transform vine by-products —skins, seeds, canes— into high-concentration functional ingredients with verifiable traceability.
que_hacemos.b3_body: We connect science with concrete industrial applications. Every ingredient we develop responds to a real demand from the food, cosmetics or nutraceutical industry.
que_hacemos.cierre: Today, one of the most advanced paths of this approach is the development and commercialization of natural colorants from grape.

tres_no.headline: Three opportunities Qualab connects
tres_no.n1_body: A significant share of the global population seeks the benefits of grape without alcohol. Qualab brings the vine's active compounds to new categories: functional foods, beverages, and supplements.
tres_no.n2_body: Skins, seeds, and stems concentrate anthocyanins, polyphenols, and high-value dietary fibers. Qualab turns them into functional ingredients with concrete industrial applications.
tres_no.n3_body: Qualab bridges viticultural research and industry: it takes validated knowledge and transforms it into solutions that reach the food, cosmetics, and nutraceutical markets.
tres_no.cierre: At the intersection of viticulture, science, and market demand, Qualab develops the ingredients industry is looking for.

cta_final.headline: Are you looking for colors or exploring natural ingredients for new applications?
cta_final.body: At Qualab we work with companies and partners interested in developing solutions of viticultural origin with scientific backing and industrial application.

colorantes_page.headline: We create the shades your consumers are looking for
colorantes_page.intro: Natural color for beverages, food and nutrition.
colorantes_page.trend: The industry is shifting to natural color: regulators are phasing out petroleum-based synthetic dyes and consumers are choosing clean labels.
colorantes_page.trend_source: Global clean label trend · FDA and international regulations
colorantes_page.gallery_body: Qualab brings to market natural colorants from plant extracts, designed for applications in food, beverages and nutraceuticals.
colorantes_page.m_blend_note: Made-to-measure color. Contact us for its technical sheet and certifications.
colorantes_page.ventajas_intro: A drying format that concentrates pigment into pure crystals, carrier-free. More color, more stable and with a cleaner label.
colorantes_page.v1_body: Pigment concentrated into crystals: intense color at very low doses and higher yield per kilo.
colorantes_page.v2_body: Up to 5 years of stability in dry storage, with no refrigeration or cold chain required.
colorantes_page.v3_body: Pure color, no carrier agents. Cleaner label, less dust in handling and simpler formulation.
colorantes_page.v4_body: Dissolves easily and is 100% natural origin, with certifications per market.
colorantes_page.industrias_body: We tailor color for the industries that need it most, by application and process.
colorantes_page.cta_title: Looking for color for your products?
colorantes_page.cta_body: Tell us your application, the shade you need and your process conditions. We'll help you find the ideal natural colorant and send you a sample.

hero.headline: An innovation platform, not just a supplier
hero.body: Qualab was born from the desire to transform a traditional industry like viticulture into a platform for innovation, expanding its impact with natural ingredients of real-world application.

proposito.headline: Qualab exists to expand the impact that viticulture can generate from those resources and knowledge that have not yet expressed their full potential.
proposito.modelo: Qualab helps food and beverage companies adopt natural ingredients of viticultural origin that work.
proposito.historia_body: Qualab was founded in 2018, within the world of viticulture, by Fernando Buscema, with the conviction that this industry could expand the impact it traditionally delivers. In its early years, the company focused on crop protection solutions. Over time, that path evolved toward developing natural ingredients. Today, Qualab connects concrete needs with viticultural ingredients that have real applications in food, beverages, functional cosmetics and preventive medicine. We remain part of the wine ecosystem — not seeking to replace it, but to complement it and expand its reach.

vision.headline: Double the impact of viticulture by transforming underutilized biomass and knowledge into natural ingredients with global adoption.
vision.body: In 3 years, become the #1 company in Latin America for commercializing ingredients of viticultural origin, with presence in Argentina, Chile and Brazil.

leo.headline: Leo: our AI to compress R&D cycles
leo.body: Leo is our artificial intelligence platform in development. It processes thousands of scientific publications and patents to identify valorization opportunities, connect compounds with biological effects and guide the development of new ingredients with robust evidence. In development.

hero.headline: Natural actives from viticultural origin
hero.subtitle: Functional solutions that elevate the nutritional profile, color and flavor of your products.
hero.body: Boost your products with the essence of the Mendoza vine: our grape powder provides fiber for digestive health and powerful antioxidants (rich in polyphenols), while grape seed oil stands out for its excellent profile of healthy unsaturated fats (high in linoleic acid / omega-6).
hero.body2: A natural synergy that raises the nutritional value of your creations, adding anti-inflammatory properties and a unique sensory appeal.

colorante.name: Natural Grape Colorant
colorante.headline: Natural grape colorants for food and beverages
colorante.intro: Qualab develops and brings to market natural colorants from grape, designed for applications in food, beverages and nutraceuticals. These colorants are based on plant extracts rich in anthocyanins, compounds naturally present in grapes and responsible for red and purple hues.
colorante.origen_body: Red grape skins from Mendoza varietals (Malbec and other red varieties). Obtained as a noble by-product of the winemaking process.
colorante.compuesto_body: Anthocyanins — the natural pigments responsible for the red-violet color of red grapes. Beyond coloring, they act as antioxidants and exhibit documented bioactive properties.
colorante.why_1: Global clean label trend: consumers reject synthetic colorants
colorante.why_3: Traceable origin and circular sustainability narrative
colorante.why_4: Alternative to carmine and tartrazine in natural formulations
colorante.cta: Let's talk about a specific application
colorante.faq_q1: Is the colorant suitable for food use?
colorante.faq_a1: Yes. It is approved under FDA 21 CFR 73.260 (USA) and complies with the NATCOL Code of Practice (EU). It also holds Kosher OU, Halal and Gluten Free certifications. Contact us about regulatory status for Argentina and other markets.
colorante.faq_a2: Yes. The technical datasheet includes the anthocyanin profile, pH and temperature stability, and recommended usage conditions. Available upon request.
colorante.faq_q3: Do you work with small volumes or pilot scale?
colorante.faq_a3: Yes, we work from pilot scale. If you are evaluating the ingredient for a new formulation, we can send a sample and support you through the process.
colorante.faq_q4: Is the colorant stable in heat or acidic pH processes?
colorante.faq_a4: Anthocyanins are most stable at acidic pH (2–4), which is the typical range for beverages and fermented products. For high-temperature processes, we recommend consulting with our technical team for specific conditions.
colorante.faq_q5: Can you send samples for evaluation?
colorante.faq_a5: Yes. We send samples for technical evaluation at your laboratory or plant. Contact us indicating your target application and the test volume you need.

aceite.name: Grape Seed Oil
aceite.headline: Grape seed oil: unique lipid profile, cold-pressed
aceite.intro: Virgin oil extracted from Mendoza grape seeds. One of the richest oils in polyunsaturated Omega-6 fatty acids, with natural vitamin E and free of chemical solvents.
aceite.origen_body: Seeds separated from post-winemaking pomace, cold-pressed to preserve their full bioactive profile — no hexane or chemical solvents used.
aceite.compuesto_body: Linoleic acid (Omega-6) — predominant at ~65–75% of the total fat profile. Accompanied by vitamin E (tocopherols) at ~20–50 mg/100 g with documented antioxidant activity, and plant sterols (~200–300 mg/100 g) with cholesterol-lowering effect.
aceite.why_1: Cold-pressed, no chemical solvents: preserves the full lipid profile without hexane or additives
aceite.why_2: ~70% polyunsaturated fatty acids (Omega-6): one of the richest oils in unsaturated fats on the market
aceite.why_3: Rich in natural vitamin E: ~28 mg of tocopherols per 100 g, with documented antioxidant activity
aceite.why_4: Cholesterol-free, trans fat-free, vegan and gluten-free: meets the main clean label standards

polvo.name: Grape Skin Powder
polvo.headline: Grape skin powder: 55% total dietary fiber with documented antioxidant activity
polvo.intro: Powder obtained from Mendoza red grape skins. 55% total dietary fiber, accompanied by 36.96 mg/g of polyphenols and 1,173 mg of anthocyanins per 100 g. Delivers nutritional functionality and bioactivity in bakery, granola and chocolate formulations.
polvo.origen_body: Red grape skins separated in the winemaking process in Mendoza, Argentina. Dehydrated and micronized (≤290 µm) to preserve active compounds. RNPA 13-063537 / RNE 13-006690 registered.
polvo.compuesto_body: Total dietary fiber — 55% content, with soluble and insoluble fractions. Acts as a functional agent in formulations: improves texture, provides satiety and enables verified nutritional claims. Accompanied by 36.96 mg/g of total polyphenols and 1,173 mg of anthocyanins per 100 g with documented antioxidant activity.
polvo.why_1: 55% total dietary fiber: enables fiber-enriched formulations with a real, verified nutritional claim
polvo.why_2: 1,173 mg of anthocyanins per 100 g: documented antioxidant activity and high-value polyphenolic profile
polvo.why_3: Health certifications: RNPA 13-063537 / RNE 13-006690 · Gluten-free (<10 ppm) · Vegan-friendly
polvo.why_4: Versatile: baked goods (5–12%), granolas, chocolates and pastries (3–8%) with B2B technical support

contacto.headline: Let's talk about your next development
contacto.body: Whether you're looking to innovate with functional ingredients, integrate plant-based natural colorants, or discover new ways to valorize viticultural biomass, we're here to help you overcome your formulation challenges.
contacto.body2: At Qualab we work side by side with companies and institutions interested in co-creating innovative solutions, offering technical support backed by science and real industrial application.
contacto.body3: Tell us what problem you're trying to solve and let's see how we can boost your products.

form.success: Message sent! We'll be in touch soon.
form.error: Something went wrong. Please write to us at informes@qualab.co
form.consent: I accept the <link>Privacy Policy</link> and the processing of my data to respond to my inquiry.

footer.tagline: We transform viticultural biomass into ingredients that improve lives.
